categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividend announcements, and articles reporting specific financial results versus analyst expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, Nasdaq/S&P 500 movements, macro data reactions, sector rotation, and articles where GOOGL is mentioned as part of a wider market or tech sector narrative.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, broker commentary, and institutional positioning or valuation milestones.

categories.label: AI & Cloud Strategy
categories.description: Gemini model launches and updates, DeepMind research breakthroughs, AI chip development, cloud partnerships with enterprise clients, AI product features, and competitive positioning in the generative AI race.

categories.label: Antitrust, Regulatory & Legal
categories.description: DOJ antitrust cases, EU fines and DMA investigations, UK competition probes, ad tech lawsuits, Play Store rulings, patent disputes, and global regulatory enforcement actions against Google.

categories.label: Data Center & Infrastructure Investment
categories.description: Global data center construction announcements, cloud region expansions, nuclear and clean energy procurement deals, submarine cable projects, and capital expenditure on physical infrastructure.

categories.label: Acquisitions & Strategic Investments
categories.description: Potential and completed acquisitions, minority equity investments, licensing deals with AI startups, divestitures, and corporate development activity.

categories.label: Waymo & Autonomous Vehicles
categories.description: Waymo service expansions, robotaxi incidents, NHTSA investigations, vehicle recalls, regulatory approvals, and autonomous driving technology developments.

categories.label: Leadership, Workforce & Operations
categories.description: C-suite appointments and departures, layoffs and restructuring, organizational changes, content moderation decisions, privacy settlements, and operational policy shifts.

dimensions.scale: 1=passing mention or tangential relevance to GOOGL fundamentals, 10=directly and substantially affects revenue, margins, or addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or confirming prior consensus, 10=outcome completely unexpected with no prior indication
1=affects only the next few trading days, 10=structural shift with implications lasting years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative sentiment
1=pure speculation, opinion, or rumor with no hard data, 10=detailed quantitative data with specific figures, comparisons, and verifiable facts
1=ambiguous implications that could be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad for GOOGL shareholders
1=affects only GOOGL specifically, 5=sector-wide implications for big tech, 10=macro or market-wide event
1=no effect on competitive positioning, 10=fundamentally reshapes competitive landscape in a key business segment
1=no regulatory or legal dimension, 10=existential regulatory threat such as forced breakup or market exit
1=transient story fully resolved within a day, 10=persistent story generating follow-up coverage for weeks or months
1=reinforces the prevailing investor thesis about GOOGL, 10=fundamentally challenges or reframes the investment thesis
1=rehash or minor update of a previously reported story, 10=first report of entirely new information
1=no financial results reported or results exactly in line with expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst consensus

kept.materiality: Ranges from GOOGL being tangentially mentioned in a market roundup or foreign policy article to DOJ breakup proposals and $23 billion acquisition talks that directly affect enterprise value.
kept.surprise: Ranges from pre-announced earnings call dates and expected partnership renewals to the unexpected DOJ breakup proposal, the Gemini image generation bias controversy, and the sudden Wiz acquisition talks.
kept.temporal_horizon: Ranges from single-day market roundups and minor fines to multi-year structural events like the DOJ antitrust remedies, DMA compliance obligations, and the AI infrastructure buildout strategy.
kept.sentiment_strength: Ranges from flat, factual partnership announcements to strongly positive coverage of the Q1 2024 earnings beat and first-ever dividend, and strongly negative coverage of the monopoly ruling and potential forced breakup.
kept.information_density: Ranges from one-line trending alerts and unverified rumors to detailed earnings releases with specific revenue, EPS, margin figures, and segment breakdowns.
kept.directional_clarity: Ranges from ambiguous articles like AI cost concerns versus growth opportunity, to clearly positive events like EU court scrapping a $1.7 billion fine, to clearly negative events like the federal judge ruling Google holds an illegal monopoly.
kept.scope: Ranges from GOOGL-only events like CFO appointment and Waymo incidents to sector-wide articles about Magnificent Seven earnings and macro pieces on Fed policy and global tax deals.
kept.competitive_impact: Ranges from routine enterprise cloud deals with no competitive shift to Gemini launch directly challenging OpenAI, the Character.AI talent acquisition, and DOJ remedies that could reshape the search market.
kept.regulatory_risk: Massive variation from zero regulatory content in partnership announcements to the DOJ contemplating a full corporate breakup, EU confirming a $2.7 billion fine, and DMA non-compliance investigations across multiple jurisdictions.
kept.expected_duration: Ranges from one-day trending mentions and market roundups to the DOJ antitrust case generating continuous coverage across the entire corpus period, and the multi-year EU regulatory saga.
kept.narrative_shift: Ranges from articles reinforcing the dominant AI-leader and advertising-cash-cow narrative to the federal monopoly ruling and breakup proposals that fundamentally reframe GOOGL as a company facing structural regulatory risk.
kept.repeatedness: High variation due to many stories having multiple updates and duplicate wire coverage versus genuine first reports like the Wiz acquisition talks, Gemini launch, and CFO hiring from Eli Lilly.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Critical for distinguishing between earnings events: Q4 2023 disappointed on cloud growth causing a 7% drop, while Q1 2024 beat expectations with 61% EPS growth triggering first-ever dividend and $2 trillion valuation, and Q3 2024 beat on cloud strength.

dropped.management_signal: Would score uniformly low for approximately 90% of articles since most coverage involves regulatory, product, or market events rather than leadership decisions. The few leadership articles are adequately captured by the leadership_workforce category and materiality dimension.
dropped.actionability: For a mega-cap stock like GOOGL with deep liquidity, nearly all substantive news is immediately actionable. This dimension would cluster most articles in the 5-8 range with insufficient variation to be discriminating.
dropped.controversy: Investor divisiveness is rare for GOOGL coverage in this corpus. Antitrust risk is already well captured by regulatory_risk, and most other stories generate consensus reactions rather than divided opinion.
